mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE falls below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% YoY, or Rank IC drops below 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects accelerating manufacturing and utility output, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive INDPRO surprises, or the signal reverses direction during monetary tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% while the unemployment rate declines, it indicates a resilient economy successfully absorbing monetary tightening, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal predicts negative returns when UNRATE rises concurrently, or out-of-sample accuracy falls below 52% over 5-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% quarter-over-quarter confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters meeting the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 20-day equity returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210.0 while the federal funds rate remains elevated, it signals persistent inflationary pressure and restrictive policy conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns when real yields decline, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 48% in walk-forward validation.
